title: Aureline run / attempt artifact-event record
description: Boundary schema for the artifact-event record every execution-shaped surface emits when a run or attempt produces, replaces, withdraws, or finalizes an artifact - whether that artifact is a streaming log chunk, a structured test report, a build binary, an AI response chunk, a network response body, a notebook cell output, a provider-job artifact, or a support-bundle segment. The artifact rail is the durable history of what an attempt produced; UI / CLI / support-export / evidence-packet consumers all read the same record family rather than inventing per-surface artifact vocabularies. Every artifact event cites a parent_attempt_ref and a parent_run_ref so per-attempt artifacts cannot be flattened across attempts; finalization is explicit (artifact_finalized_published is the typed event), withdrawal cites the typed reason, and a finalized artifact MUST NOT be silently overwritten - a replacement mints a new artifact_event_record with artifact_event_kind_class = artifact_withdrawn_replaced_by_newer on the prior artifact and artifact_finalized_published on the replacement. Vocabularies compose with - and never re-mint - the lifecycle / queue admission / host boundary / redaction / failure / denial / audit vocabularies frozen in schemas/execution/run.schema.json and schemas/execution/attempt.schema.json, the task-event envelope artifact_publication_payload in schemas/tooling/task_event_envelope.schema.json, the credential-handle classes in the secret-broker boundary, and the record-class registry in artifacts/governance/record_class_registry.yaml. Raw artifact bytes, raw stdout / stderr byte streams, raw env bodies, raw API bodies, raw absolute paths, raw URLs, and raw secret values MUST NOT appear in any record; the artifact body itself rides out-of-band retention and the record carries only opaque ids, hashes, counts, and class labels. Adding a new enum member, a new optional field, or a new sub-record kind is additive-minor with an artifact_event_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing member is breaking and requires a new decision row.

artifact_event_schema_version.description: Document-level schema version. Bumped on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields and new enum members do not bump this version.

hex_hash.description: Hash-algorithm-prefixed digest (for example 'sha256:7b9f0b2d...') of the raw artifact body. The raw bytes themselves never cross this boundary.

artifact_event_kind_class.description: Closed artifact-event-kind vocabulary. Streaming and one-shot emissions are distinct; finalization is explicit. Withdrawal cites the typed authority (replaced_by_newer / by_user / by_policy / by_quarantine). artifact_failed_to_publish is the typed honest answer for an emission that never reached its retention surface; artifact_retained_under_redaction_only is the typed honest answer when only metadata and hashes survived the redaction-class boundary. artifact_pending_finalization is the honest mid-state for a streaming artifact that has not received its finalize event yet.

artifact_class.description: Closed artifact-class vocabulary. Names what kind of artifact the event refers to without mandating any content shape. Streaming chunk classes (log_stream_chunk, console_output_chunk, ai_response_message_chunk) are emitted with artifact_emitted_streaming_chunk; one-shot classes (test report, build binary, coverage report, etc.) are emitted with artifact_emitted_complete_one_shot. artifact_class_unknown_requires_review fails closed.

finalization_class.description: Closed finalization-class vocabulary. The contract guarantee is that a finalized artifact (finalization_published_immutable) MUST NOT be silently overwritten; replacement requires a typed withdraw event on the prior artifact and a new finalize event on the replacement.

retention_class.description: Re-export of the raw_payload_retention_class vocabulary from schemas/tooling/task_event_envelope.schema.json. retained_managed_with_broadened_capture requires an approval-ticket ref.

media_class.description: Closed media-class vocabulary for the artifact body. Re-export of the raw_payload_media_class set from schemas/tooling/task_event_envelope.schema.json with binary classes added for build / coverage / image artifacts.

side_effect_attribution_class.description: Closed side-effect attribution vocabulary for an artifact event. Composes with the run-level side_effect_summary_class on schemas/execution/run.schema.json: a run's outcome rolls up the per-artifact attributions. side_effect_irreversible_action_published_attributable_to_artifact MUST narrow the run record's auto_rerun_admitted_on_this_record to false.

artifact_ref.description: Opaque artifact id. Stable across streaming chunks for the same artifact - one logical artifact may receive many artifact_emitted_streaming_chunk events under the same artifact_ref before its artifact_finalized_published event.

chunk_index.description: Zero-based index for streaming chunks. Required when artifact_event_kind_class is artifact_emitted_streaming_chunk; null otherwise.

is_final_chunk.description: True when this streaming chunk is the last chunk of the artifact. Null for non-streaming events. A streaming artifact does not become finalized until a separate artifact_finalized_published event is emitted.

replacement_artifact_ref.description: Required when artifact_event_kind_class is artifact_withdrawn_replaced_by_newer. Null otherwise.

withdrawal_actor_ref.description: Required when artifact_event_kind_class is artifact_withdrawn_by_user (cite the command-dispatch descriptor), artifact_withdrawn_by_policy (cite the admin-policy epoch), or artifact_withdrawn_by_quarantine (cite the quarantine row). Null otherwise.

raw_artifact_body_ref.description: Opaque retention id pointing at the raw artifact body. Required when retention_class is one of the retained_* values. MUST be null when retention_class is not_retained_ephemeral.

raw_artifact_bytes_reported.description: Raw artifact size in bytes. Null for streaming chunks before the size is known.

raw_artifact_hash.description: Digest of the raw artifact body. Required for finalization_published_immutable and finalization_published_replaced_by_newer when raw_artifact_bytes_reported is non-null and non-zero.

credential_handle_class_refs.description: Refs to credential-handle classes intersected by the artifact body. Required (non-empty) when redaction_class is broadened_capture and the artifact intersects credential-handle classes; the artifact MUST NOT publish raw secret values regardless of this list.

approval_ticket_ref.description: Required when retention_class is retained_managed_with_broadened_capture, when redaction_class is broadened_capture, or when artifact_class is exact_build_identity_attestation.

side_effect_attribution_note.description: Optional sentence amplifying the side-effect attribution. Raw URLs, raw paths, raw API bodies, and raw secret values MUST NOT appear here.

task_event_envelope_event_id.description: Optional ref to the task_event_envelope_record event_id that emitted the matching artifact_publication payload. Lets a stable consumer correlate the run-level artifact rail with the lower-level task-event stream without flattening either model.

summary.description: Optional reviewer-facing summary safe for export. Raw artifact bytes, raw paths, raw URLs, raw API bodies, and raw secret material MUST NOT appear here.

allOf.description: Streaming chunk events MUST carry chunk_index and is_final_chunk; non-streaming events MUST null both.
artifact_withdrawn_replaced_by_newer MUST cite the replacement artifact ref.
artifact_withdrawn_by_user / by_policy / by_quarantine MUST cite a withdrawal actor and resolve finalization to withdrawn.
artifact_finalized_published MUST resolve finalization to published_immutable and is non-streaming.
Retained-* retention classes MUST cite a non-null raw_artifact_body_ref; not_retained_ephemeral MUST null it.
retained_managed_with_broadened_capture MUST cite an approval-ticket ref.
broadened_capture redaction MUST cite an approval-ticket ref.
exact_build_identity_attestation MUST cite an approval-ticket ref to keep the attestation rail attestable.

artifact_event_record.description: One artifact event. Append-only entry on the per-attempt artifact rail. A streaming artifact accumulates chunks under one artifact_ref and is finalized only when an explicit artifact_finalized_published event is emitted; finalized artifacts MUST NOT be silently overwritten.
